---
title: "Solid Propellant Rocket Motors: Internal Ballistics"
subtitle: "Theory ⋅ Equations ⋅ Numerical Simulation"
author: "Troy Altus"
date: today
---

# Welcome

This book presents a technical deep-dive into the **internal ballistics** of solid propellant rocket motors — from fundamental combustion physics to practical design and simulation methods.

## What This Book Covers

**Fundamentals**
- Propellant chemistry and combustion
- Burning rate laws and scaling effects
- Solid grain geometry and design principles

**Core Internal Ballistics**
- Chamber pressure prediction
- Mass generation and flow balance
- Thrust curve generation
- Specific impulse and performance metrics

**Advanced Topics**
- Transient behavior and ignition
- Erosive burning and stability
- Numerical modeling techniques

## Who This Book Is For

- Amateur and experimental rocketeers
- Aerospace engineering students
- Hobbyists building solid rocket motors
- Anyone interested in the science behind solid propulsion

## How to Use This Book

- Navigate using the sidebar
- Equations are rendered with LaTeX
- Code examples (when added) can be executed interactively
- All chapters are designed to build progressively

::: {.callout-tip}
This is a living document. New chapters, examples, and simulation code will be added regularly.
:::

**Feedback and contributions are welcome.**

---

**Last updated:** `r format(Sys.Date(), "%B %Y")`
